findingDetails.title: Goal-plugin docs lack a single contract owner and canonical output-field schema
findingDetails.claim: The documentation topology itself makes future goal-plugin contract changes drift-prone because env vars are documented in multiple places while output fields have no canonical schema section.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: A single authoritative mk-goal operator-contract doc listing both env vars and status/output fields; none was found in the reviewed docs.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: This could be a one-off propagation miss by the implementer rather than a topology problem, but the same code change drifted across env docs, output docs, delegation docs, and playbooks, while output fields have no canonical section.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to note/no-finding if goal_plugin.md gains a dedicated status-output schema and README/plugin README delegation is corrected to point to it.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Read coverage included ENV_REFERENCE.md, goal_plugin.md, root README, .opencode/plugins/README.md, feature catalog, two playbooks, and mk-goal.js output renderer.
findingDetails.recommendation: Make goal_plugin.md the canonical operator contract, add an Output Fields table aligned with goalStateLines, and convert other docs to pointers or validation-only references.

traceabilityChecks.codeSelfDocumentation: adequate; output rendering centralized in goalStateLines

title: Goal-plugin docs lack a single contract owner and canonical output-field schema

name: doc-structure-root-cause
notes: Duplicated env tables plus missing output-field owner strengthen P1-001/P1-002/P1-003 without re-emitting them.
Retention helper names and goalStateLines centralization make code behavior inferable; documentation topology remains weaker than code.

reason: No new P0/P1 findings; one advisory P2 maintainability finding.
